title: Mushroom vol-au-vents
subtitle: Puff pastry cases filled with a creamy garlic and thyme mushroom sauce
excerpt: Ready-made puff pastry baked into hollow cases, filled with a quick creamy mushroom sauce. A classic party and buffet pastry. Makes about 12.
sourceNotes: Vol-au-vents, small hollow puff pastry cases with savoury fillings, are documented in French household patisserie from the nineteenth century. Auguste Escoffier, Le Guide Culinaire (1903), includes them among the standard hot entrées. The mushroom cream filling is a standard French household preparation documented in household guides from the same period. The vol-au-vent became a British buffet and party standard during the 1960s and 1970s.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry cases can be baked a day ahead and stored in a tin. Fill just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 210°C in a conventional oven

glossaryTerms.definition: A vol-au-vent case is made by cutting two circles from puff pastry and stacking them. The base circle is left whole; the top circle has a smaller circle cut from its centre to form a ring. Egg wash goes only on the top ring — not on the sides, which would glue the layers together and prevent them rising. In the oven, the layered sides rise dramatically while the base stays flat. After baking, the inner disc on the base is pressed down to form a hollow for the filling.

content.text: The only tricky part is not over-egg-washing the sides. Egg wash on the cut edge glues the layers and stops the rise. Brush the top surface only. The
bakes itself hollow — the filling goes in only after the pastry is out of the oven and cooled slightly.
Cut and bake the cases — 20 minutes

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll the {{puff-pastry}} to 4 mm thick on a lightly floured surface. Cut 24 circles with an 8 cm cutter. Cut a 5 cm circle from the centre of 12 of the circles to make rings. Place the whole circles on a lined baking tray. Top each with a ring, pressing lightly to adhere. Brush the top surface of the rings only with beaten {{eggs}} — avoid the sides. Bake for 18–20 minutes until risen and deep gold. Press the inner disc down with a teaspoon to form the hollow. Cool on a rack.

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a wide pan over medium heat. Soften the {{shallot}} and {{garlic}} for 3 minutes. Add the {{mushrooms-chestnut}} and {{thyme-fresh}} and cook, stirring, until all the liquid has evaporated and the mushrooms are beginning to colour — about 8 minutes. Pour in the {{double-cream}} and simmer for 3–4 minutes until the sauce is thick enough to mound on a spoon. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Spoon the filling into the warm or room-temperature cases and serve immediately.
